reviews.review_msg: Class was fairly easy but exams were a bit unreasonable and would test irrelevant material. She could not use web ct to post marks and it would take her sometimes two weeks to post exam marks. The exams were multiple choice and should not have taken so long. i also found her very annoying

It is true that she plays favorites, but anyone can get on her good side if you show you care about the subject.  Despite being a hard worker, she always finds time to answer questions.  One of the few professors who genuinely cares about her students.  She is also a great reference if youre planning to work in BC!

awesome awesome proff! I see no reason why anyone reasonable would get on her bad side. Probably one of my absolute favourites. Incredibly helpful, and wants to see you succeed. I was skeptical of the evaluation method in the beginning, but i'm converted, one of the best classes i've taken. Really effective lecturer too. wish i could take more!

Dana was such a sweetheart! If you read the chapters she assigned before class, you'll have no trouble following in class. She focuses a lot on archaeological sites - read about those a lot! Her slides were easy to follow and usually interesting and her exams were relatively easy too.
Good professor, cares about her students

Dana is a great communicator and has a passion for her work and for her students' wellbeing that really comes through in her lectures and any one-on-one discussions. A bit too easy of a marker, though.
Tempramental. You MUST stay on her good side. Plays favorites.
Horrible, made no sense, maybe its just archaeology that makes no sense, but she did nothing to help the matter, field of complete and utter bull
